summary: Pillar CMS Pages refactoring tested successfully. Both Paperwork and Stay pages now load CMS-driven configuration with Ask Mira section at top. All backend API endpoints return valid data. Product modals work correctly on Paperwork page.

issues: Some bundles show 'Save NaN' badge indicating missing original_price calculation

backend_tests.GET /api/paperwork/page-config: PASSED - Returns config with title '{petName}', askMira, sections, documentCategories (6)
backend_tests.GET /api/stay/page-config: PASSED - Returns config (uses generic CMS endpoint)

frontend_tests.paperwork_page_loads: PASSED - Page loads with CMS-driven title 'Keep your pet's world in order'
frontend_tests.paperwork_ask_mira_section: PASSED - Ask Mira search bar visible at top with placeholder 'Find vaccination records...'
frontend_tests.paperwork_document_kits: PASSED - Document Organisation Kits section visible with bundles
frontend_tests.paperwork_product_modals: PASSED - Clicking product opens modal with name, description, price, Add to Cart
frontend_tests.stay_page_loads: PASSED - Page loads with title 'Find the perfect stay for your pet'
frontend_tests.stay_ask_mira_section: PASSED - Ask Mira search bar visible at top
frontend_tests.stay_filters: PASSED - Filter dropdowns visible (city, property type, rating)

verified_features: Paperwork page CMS-driven title with {petName} template
Paperwork page Ask Mira section at top with search input
Paperwork page Document Organisation Kits section with bundles
Paperwork page Individual Products section with product cards
Paperwork page product modal opens on click with Add to Cart button
Stay page CMS integration added with Ask Mira at top
Stay page generic CMS endpoint /api/stay/page-config working
API routes registered correctly - pillar-specific before generic
action_items: Consider seeding default CMS config for Stay page (currently returns empty config)
Fix 'Save NaN' badge on some paperwork bundles - missing original_price calculation
seed_data_creation: None required - existing CMS data used
context_for_next_testing_agent: Paperwork and Stay pillar pages have been refactored to be CMS-driven. Paperwork uses dedicated /api/paperwork/page-config route with document categories. Stay uses generic /api/{pillar}/page-config route. Both have Ask Mira section at top. Product modals work on Paperwork page. Minor issue: some bundles show 'Save NaN' badge.
